description: Trigger discrimination for the designs-vs-measures seam: the measurement instrument (this skill) vs traffic planning (load-test-planner, the sibling), designing FOR performance (latency-budget-architect / frontend-perf-engineer — D12.3), production SLO monitoring (slo-reliability-architect), and functional automation architecture (qa-automation-architect).

cases.prompt: Build the performance regression detection: baselines per endpoint and key page, repeat-run comparison against noise, thresholds pulled from our existing budgets, and gates in CI per PR and nightly.
cases.reason: Baselines, variance-aware comparison, threshold consumption, and CI gate placement are the measurement instrument's whole anatomy. The budgets already exist (consumed, not set), and no workload modeling is requested.

cases.prompt: Model the traffic for the big test: realistic endpoint mix from production logs, a tenant mix with two whale tenants and a thousand small ones, ramp profiles, and a 12-hour soak plus a spike scenario.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (sibling) — workload models, tenant mix, ramps, and soak/spike scenario design are the traffic PLAN. The harness is the instrument that executes such plans and measures the result; nothing here designs measurement, baselines, or gates.
cases.reason: Everything requested is scenario/workload realism — the planner's scope; the harness would have nothing to add until this plan exists.

cases.prompt: Allocate our 400ms p95 target across the gateway, services, and database, and derive the timeouts from the split.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (designs-vs-measures, primary) — allocating the target and deriving timeouts is engineering FOR performance (D12.3). The harness later ASSERTS these budgets as gate thresholds; it never performs the allocation.
cases.reason: Budget decomposition is design-side work whose output becomes the harness's cited thresholds — the direction of consumption is the seam.

cases.prompt: Define what we promise users for availability and latency in production, with error budgets and burn-rate alerts that page the on-call.
cases.reason: Production targets, error budgets, and paging are the SLO catalog — continuous production reliability governance. The harness is PRE-release validation evidence; it consumes SLO targets as thresholds but owns no production alerting.

cases.prompt: Design our test automation architecture: runner choice per layer, fixture and factory structure, parallelization, and what runs on PR versus nightly for the functional suites.
cases.reason: Functional suite architecture (runners, fixtures, parallelization, CI placement for correctness tests) is qa-automation-architect's blueprint. The perf harness is a sibling instrument with its own variance/environment disciplines — 'what runs on PR vs nightly' alone does not route here when the subject is functional suites.
